title: DogStatsD Metrics Emission Flow
interaction_flow: PHPApplication → DDTrace PHP Extension: dogstatsd_count(string $metric, int $value, array $tags = []): void
PHPApplication → DDTrace PHP Extension: dogstatsd_gauge(string $metric, float $value, array $tags = []): void
PHPApplication → DDTrace PHP Extension: dogstatsd_histogram(string $metric, float $value, array $tags = []): void
PHPApplication → DDTrace PHP Extension: dogstatsd_distribution(string $metric, float $value, array $tags = []): void
PHPApplication → DDTrace PHP Extension: dogstatsd_set(string $metric, int $value, array $tags = []): void
DDTrace PHP Extension → SidecarLayer: ddtrace_sidecar_dogstatsd_count(zend_string *metric, zend_long value, zval *tags)
DDTrace PHP Extension → SidecarLayer: ddtrace_sidecar_dogstatsd_gauge(zend_string *metric, double value, zval *tags)
DDTrace PHP Extension → SidecarLayer: ddtrace_sidecar_dogstatsd_histogram(zend_string *metric, double value, zval *tags)
DDTrace PHP Extension → SidecarLayer: ddtrace_sidecar_dogstatsd_distribution(zend_string *metric, double value, zval *tags)
DDTrace PHP Extension → SidecarLayer: ddtrace_sidecar_dogstatsd_set(zend_string *metric, zend_long value, zval *tags)
SidecarLayer → SidecarLayer: guard check — if (!ddtrace_sidecar || !get_DD_INTEGRATION_METRICS_ENABLED()) return early
SidecarLayer → SidecarLayer: ddog_Vec_Tag_new() — create tag vector
SidecarLayer → SidecarLayer: ddtrace_sidecar_push_tags(&vec, tags) — iterate PHP array, convert key/value to ddog_CharSlice
SidecarLayer → ddog FFI: ddog_sidecar_dogstatsd_count(&ddtrace_sidecar, ddtrace_sidecar_instance_id, metric_slice, value, &vec)
SidecarLayer → ddog FFI: ddog_sidecar_dogstatsd_gauge(...)
SidecarLayer → ddog FFI: ddog_sidecar_dogstatsd_histogram(...)
SidecarLayer → ddog FFI: ddog_sidecar_dogstatsd_distribution(...)
SidecarLayer → ddog FFI: ddog_sidecar_dogstatsd_set(...)
SidecarLayer → SidecarLayer: ddog_Vec_Tag_drop(vec) — free tag vector
ddog FFI → Datadog Agent: UDP/UDS datagram — format: metric:value|type[|@sample_rate][|#tags,const_tags]
PHPApplication → DDTrace PHP Extension: add_global_tag(string $key, string $value): void [optional, applied to all spans AND global const_tags]
key_relationships: PHPApplication calls DDTrace\dogstatsd_* functions (DDTrace namespace, ext/ddtrace.stub.php)
PHP_FUNCTION(DDTrace_dogstatsd_count) delegates to ddtrace_sidecar_dogstatsd_count() in sidecar.c
sidecar.c calls ddog FFI (Rust libdatadog) for actual metric forwarding
ddog Rust sidecar process owns the dogstatsd_endpoint (UDP or Unix socket)
dogstatsd_endpoint initialized from DD_DOGSTATSD_URL or DD_API_KEY (agentless mode)
ddtrace_sidecar_instance_id ties metrics to the PHP runtime instance (session_id + runtime_id)
add_global_tag (DDTrace\add_global_tag) adds global tags applied to every span; Tracer.php calls it for config['global_tags']

metric_types_and_signatures.count: dogstatsd_count(string $metric, int $value, array $tags = []): void — StatsD type 'c'
metric_types_and_signatures.gauge: dogstatsd_gauge(string $metric, float $value, array $tags = []): void — StatsD type 'g'
metric_types_and_signatures.histogram: dogstatsd_histogram(string $metric, float $value, array $tags = []): void — StatsD type 'h'
metric_types_and_signatures.distribution: dogstatsd_distribution(string $metric, float $value, array $tags = []): void — StatsD type 'd'
metric_types_and_signatures.set: dogstatsd_set(string $metric, int $value, array $tags = []): void — StatsD type 's'

wire_format: metric_name:value|type[|@sample_rate][|#tag1,tag2,...,const_tags] — sent via sendto() UDP SOCK_DGRAM or PF_UNIX socket
guard_conditions: If either check fails, function returns silently with no error
initialization_flow: ddtrace_set_non_resettable_sidecar_globals() sets dogstatsd_endpoint from ddtrace_dogstatsd_url() or DD_API_KEY
dd_sidecar_post_connect() calls ddog_sidecar_session_set_config() passing dogstatsd_endpoint to the Rust sidecar
ddtrace_sidecar_instance_id built from session_id + runtime_id via ddog_sidecar_instanceId_build()

c_client_layer.note: src/dogstatsd/ contains a standalone C DogStatsD client library used for direct metric sending (not the primary path for PHP API calls)
c_client_layer.dogstatsd_client_metric_send: formats StatsD datagram as '%s:%s|%s[|@%.6f]|#tags' then calls sendto() on UDP socket
c_client_layer.inline_wrappers: dogstatsd_client_count/gauge/histogram all call dogstatsd_client_metric_send with appropriate dogstatsd_metric_t and sample_rate=1.0

important_notes: The primary emission path in PHP is: PHP userland → C extension (ddtrace.c) → sidecar.c → ddog Rust FFI → Datadog Agent via UDS/UDP. The src/dogstatsd C client library is a lower-level utility. DD_INTEGRATION_METRICS_ENABLED controls whether metrics are emitted. add_global_tag() applies to APM spans (not DogStatsD tags). Per-call tags are passed as PHP arrays and converted to ddog_Vec_Tag. Namespace: DDTrace\.
